questions.text: Which one of the following compounds is not known?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Antimony trichloride is an inorganic chloride salt with formula SbCl3. It is used as a reagent for detecting vitamin A and related carotenoids, reacting with the carotenoid to form a blue complex that can be measured by colorimetry.

In case of Nitrogen, NCl3 is possible but not NCl5, whereas in case of P, PCl3 as well as PCl5 is possible, this is because: Availability of vacant d orbital in P but not in N. Lower electronegativity of P than N. Lower tendency of H bond formation in P than N.
Nitrogen triiodide is the inorganic compound with the formula NI3. It is an extremely sensitive contact explosive.

questions.text: Which of the following is used at the tips of match stick:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The head of a match uses antimony trisulfide for fuel. Wax helps the flame travel down the matchstick and glue holds all the stuff together.

questions.text: Which element is the most abundant in the earth’s crust:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Iron is the most abundant element, by mass, in the Earth, constituting about 80% of the inner and outer cores of Earth. The molten outer core is about 8000 km in diameter, and the solid inner core is about 2400 km in diameter. Iron is the fourth most abundant element in Earth's crust

Oxygen is by far the most abundant element in the Earth's crust, making up 46% of mass—coming up just short of half of the total. Oxygen is a highly reactive element that combines with other elements, forming oxides.

Silicon makes up 27.7% of the Earth's crust by mass and is the second most abundant element (oxygen is the first). It does not occur uncombined in nature but occurs chiefly as the oxide (silica) and as silicates.
Although widely distributed in nature, carbon is not particularly plentiful—it makes up only about 0.025 percent of Earth's crust—yet it forms more compounds than all the other elements combined..

questions.explanation: Oxygen is by far the most abundant element in the Earth's crust, making up 46% of mass—coming up just short of half of the total. Oxygen is a highly reactive element that combines with other elements, forming oxides.

questions.text: SO3 is not absorbed in water directly to form H2SO4 because:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: The reaction does not go to completion
options.explanation: SO3 dissolution in water is extremely exothermic. This forces the sulfuric acid to form a mist of tiny droplets.
